summary: Iteration 10 — tier price bump to Starter $79 / Pro $199 / Bundle $399, Free tier card removed, Emergent badge hidden. Backend: all 41 pytest tests pass (updated test_tiers.py and test_backend.py for new amounts). /api/checkout/mock returns amount 79/199/399 for the 3 tiers with credits 1/1/5; default/invalid tier still falls back to 'pro' (now $199); 402 gating and classification tiers unchanged. Frontend verified on the preview URL: Pricing section renders exactly 3 cards (pricing-starter-card/$79, pricing-pro-card/$199 'Most popular' inverted-black, pricing-bundle-card/$399); pricing-free-card not in DOM. CTAs (pricing-{tier}-cta) navigate to /quiz?tier=<id> and persist sessionStorage.preferred_tier. Landing body contains zero 'Made with Emergent', 'Book a call', 'hello@roys-enterprise.com', '$29' or '$149' occurrences. Emergent badge (#emergent-badge) exists in DOM but is_visible=False (display:none applied). Hero CTA 'from $79' present. ResultsPage (unpaid, high-risk) shows tier-ladder sidebar with $79 / $199 / $399 and 'Pick a tier · from $79' button. MockCheckoutModal: tier-option-starter=$79, tier-option-pro=$199 POPULAR, tier-option-bundle=$399; pay button label updates correctly: 'PAY $79 · STARTER (MOCK)' → 'PAY $399 · BUNDLE (MOCK)' → 'PAY $79 · STARTER (MOCK)'. JSON-LD offers array on landing contains '79', '199', '399'. FAQ contains the new law-firm-audit question. End-to-end quiz→results→mock-checkout flow works via the public URL.

issues: /app/frontend/src/components/Hero.jsx line 110 still reads 'No signup required for the free tier.' — stale copy now that the Free tier has been removed. Should be rephrased (e.g., 'No signup required to see your risk classification.') for price-narrative consistency.

action_items: Update Hero.jsx microcopy: remove the trailing 'for the free tier.' now that the Free tier no longer exists.
(Carry-over from iter 9) Consider hiding the tier-ladder on PAID ResultsPage; currently only its CTA hides.
(Carry-over) server.py /api/checkout/mock silently coerces invalid tier → 'pro'. Consider returning 400 to surface client bugs (now even more impactful because price jumped from $79 → $199).
critical_code_review_comments: Pricing.jsx: CTA labels hardcode the USD amount ('Start quiz · unlock $79'). Lift tier prices to a single source (PRICING_TIERS export or /api/pricing) so price changes don't require editing 6+ components (Pricing, MockCheckoutModal, ExitIntentModal, Hero, ResultsPage tier-ladder, FAQ, Landing JSON-LD).

MockCheckoutModal.jsx duplicates the tier price list that already exists in backend TIER_PRICING and frontend Pricing TIERS. Expose a /api/pricing endpoint and fetch once on mount to eliminate the 3-way drift risk.
Hero.jsx: 'No signup required for the free tier.' is contradictory after Free tier removal — active bug in messaging.
Landing.jsx JSON-LD offers array is inlined; refactor to map over the same PRICING_TIERS constant to prevent SEO/price drift.
Emergent-badge hide uses both inline style and CSS safety net — good. Consider also aria-hidden="true" for screen-reader parity.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on checked assertions (~25 boolean/string checks across pricing, modal, results, JSON-LD, badge, forbidden terms)

seed_data_creation: ~7 additional TEST_-prefixed quiz sessions + 1 paid (high-risk tier=starter via end-to-end UI flow) created against preview URL during this run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: TIER_PRICING in backend/server.py: starter=79/1cr, pro=199/1cr, bundle=399/5cr. /api/checkout/mock still silently falls back to 'pro' for missing/invalid tier (now $199). Frontend price is duplicated across: Pricing.jsx TIERS, MockCheckoutModal.jsx tiers array, ExitIntentModal footer, Hero CTA text, ResultsPage.jsx tier-ladder (lines 189-191), Landing.jsx JSON-LD offers, FAQ copy, Testimonials. Emergent badge hidden via inline display:none in public/index.html + index.css #emergent-badge rule — element still present in DOM (is_visible=False). 'Roy's Enterprise' brand name in Footer/Landing is the company brand, NOT the removed Enterprise tier card. Only remaining stale-copy hit: Hero.jsx line 110 mentions 'the free tier'.
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs. Stale assertions in test_tiers.py (amounts 29/79/149) and test_backend.py (pay amount 79 expectation) were updated to match the new TIER_PRICING map (79/199/399).
